findings.title: Unsupported `cook` actions silently clear the deferred solvency check

findings.title: A zero oracle rate is treated as valid, making dust-collateralized debt appear solvent and unliquidatable

best_agent_match.title: A zero oracle rate is treated as valid, making dust-collateralized debt appear fully solvent

findings.title: `init()` ignores the oracle success flag and can seed a poisoned cached price at deployment

best_agent_match.title: `init()` ignores the oracle success flag and can seed a poisoned cached price

findings.title: Borrowing, collateral withdrawals, and liquidation decisions continue against stale prices when oracle updates fail

best_agent_match.title: Risk actions continue against stale cached prices whenever the oracle update fails

rejected_candidates.title: Public clone initialization is first-come-first-served if deployment is ever non-atomic
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on an out-of-scope, non-atomic clone deployment flow that is not shown in the in-scope code. The available interface strongly suggests clone deployment takes init data in the same transaction, so the reported race is too speculative to keep as a protocol finding.
